program_en: B.B.A. (Integrated BBA Programme) and Juris Doctor
program_chi: 工商管理學士（工商管理學士綜合課程）及法律博士
learning_outcomes:
BAJDN_Outcomes_ENG 1. Major Programme: Upon completion of the BBA
programme, students will have acquired: 1. effective business
communication skills (S); 2. effective analytical skills
which support them in making business decisions (S); 3. the ability to integrate
business knowledge and solve problems in a business environment (K/S); 4. the ability to propose
business strategies in a global environment (S); 5. awareness of the ethical
issues in a business context (V); 6. an understanding of the
legal landscape in which business is conducted (K). 2. JD Programme: Upon completion of the JD
programme, students will have acquired: 1. an understanding of the law
and legal system of Hong Kong, with particular emphasis on the law in action and
the dynamic interplay between law and other social phenomena. (K, V) 2. an appreciation of the
common law system and its values, and its interaction with the law of China,
the East Asian region and the wider world. (K, V) 3. a strong sense of ethics,
civic duty, social and professional responsibility. (V) 4. awareness of the strengths
and weaknesses of law as a means of regulating society in the context of competing
and conflicting interests. (K, V) 5. acquired and improved
skills of legal analysis and reasoning; skills of legal research; skills of
oral and written expression of legal ideas; and the general critical and
evaluative faculties appropriate to a postgraduate degree in law. (S) K = Knowledge outcomes; S
= Skills outcomes; V = Values outcomes Note: The expected learning
outcomes for the JD programme are only available in English.
study_scheme:
Bachelor of Business Administration (Integrated BBA Programme) and Juris
Doctor Bachelor of Business
Administration (Integrated BBA Programme) and Juris Doctor Applicable to students admitted
in 2024-25
1st Degree:
Bachelor of Business Administration (Integrated BBA Programme)
Major
Programme Requirement
Students are
required to complete a minimum of 70 units of courses as follows:
Units
1.  |  Faculty Package:  |  9
DOTE1030, 1040, MGNT1020
2.  |  Required Courses:  |  28
ACCT2111, 2121, DOTE2011, 2030, 2051, FINA2010, IBBA3040, MGNT2511, 2512,
4010 (capstone course), MKTG2010
3.  |  Law Courses[a]:  |  18
LAWS1020, 1030,
1041, 1042, 2131, 2132
4.  |  Elective Courses
(Concentration):  |  15-18
Students must
choose one concentration and take five or six courses among the courses
prescribed under respective concentration areas as
follows:
(a)  |  Business
Economics
(i)  |  DOTE2021, 4110;
(ii)  |  two courses
selected from: DOTE3000, 3011, 3030, 3050, 3080, 3090, 4040, 4080; and
(iii)  |  one DOTE course
at 3000 or above level, excluding the courses taken for fulfillment of requirement (i) or (ii)
(b)  |  Business
Analytics
(i)  |  DOTE2021, 2040, 4020;
(ii)  |  one course
selected from: DOTE4070, 4240, 4260; and
(iii)  |  one course
selected from: DOTE3030, 4030, 4110, 4220, 4280, MKTG4120
(c)  |  Finance
(i)  |  DOTE2021 or
FINA2020; and
(ii)  |  15 units of FINA courses at 3000 or above level, with no more than
three 1-unit FINA courses
(d)  |  Entrepreneurship
(i)  |  MGNT1070, 2070; and
(ii)  |  four courses
selected from: MGNT3080, 4070, 4090, 4130, 4160, 4170, 4270, 4570, 4711,
4712, 4713
(e)  |  Management of
International Business
(i)  |  MGNT3580, 4150; and
(ii)  |  four courses selected from: MGNT3010, 3080, 3100,
4080, 4090, 4110, 4130, 4140, 4510, 4530, 4540, 4550, 4570
(f)  |  Human Resource
Management
(i)  |  MGNT2040, 3010; and
(ii)  |  four courses
selected from: MGNT3040, 3060, 3090, 3100, 4050, 4060, 4080, 4110, 4130,
4140
(g)  |  Marketing
(i)  |  MKTG3010, 3020,
3030, 4040; and
(ii)  |  two courses
selected from: MKTG3040, 3050, 4010, 4020, 4030, 4050, 4070, 4080, 4090,
4110, 4160, 4200
(h)  |  Big Data and Quantitative
Marketing
(i)  |  MKTG3010, 3060, 4080,
4090; and
(ii)  |  two courses
selected from: MKTG3020, 4030, 4050, 4120, 4150, 4160, 4170, 4180, 4190,
4200
(i)  |  General Business
(i)  |  3 units of DOTE/FINA/MGNT/MKTG courses at 2000 or above level; and
(ii)  |  12 units of DOTE/FINA/MGNT/MKTG
courses at 3000 or above level,
excluding the courses taken
for fulfillment of requirement (i), with no more than three 1-unit FINA courses
Total:  |  70-73[b]
Explanatory Notes:
1.  |  ACCT/DOTE/FINA/IBBA/MGNT/MKTG courses at 2000 and above level
(excluding ACCT2111, 2121, IBBA3040,
MGNT2511 and 2512) will be
included in the calculation of Major GPA for honours classification.
2.  |  Students
claiming Entrepreneurship Concentration are not allowed to declare Minor
in Entrepreneurship and Innovation.
3.  |  In addition to
fulfilling the major programme requirement of the 1st degree,
students are advised to take MGNT2611 as an elective course.
[a]  |  Students who
have completed 18 units of LAWS courses will be exempted from 9 units of
the equivalent courses in the Juris Doctor (JD) programme.
[b]  |  Students who
choose one of the following concentrations: (i) Finance, (ii) Entrepreneurship, (iii) Management of International Business, (iv)
Human Resource Management, (v) Marketing, or (vi) Big Data and Quantitative
Marketing are required to complete a minimum of 73 units in order to
fulfill the Major programme requirement.
2nd Degree: Juris Doctor
Programme Requirement
Students are
required to complete a minimum of 72 units of courses as follows:
Units
1.  |  Required Courses:  |  15 or 18
LAWS6001[a],
6004, 6006[b], 6007, 6901 or 6902[c]
2.  |  Elective Courses:  |  54 or 57
Students are
required to take at least 54 or 57 units of courses from the following[d]:
-  |  Elective courses
which are pre-requisites for subsequent admission to the Postgraduate
Certificate in Laws (PCLL) Programme: LAWS6011, 6013, 6015, 6017, 6018,
6019, 6020, 6021[e], 6022, 6023[f], 6024
-  |  Other elective
courses offered under the JD programme
-  |  Elective courses
offered under the Master of Laws programmes
Total:  |  72[g]
Explanatory Notes:
1.  |  To be eligible
to proceed to the fifth year for the JD degree, students must achieve a
minimum CGPA (to be specified by the Faculty of Law) after completion of
the fourth year. Students may choose to opt out from the double degree
programme and complete a BBA degree or a BBA degree with a Minor in Law
in four years, subject to the fulfillment of the
requirements specified in the relevant study scheme.
[a]  |  Students who
have completed LAWS1020 and 1030 in their first three years of studies in
the BBA-JD programme will be exempted from LAWS6001.
[b]  |  Students may
take LAWS6089 to substitute LAWS6006.
[c]  |  Students are
required to take either LAWS6901 (3-unit course) or 6902 (6-unit course).
The extra 3 units completed in LAWS6902 will be counted towards the total
units required for graduation.
[d]  |  LAWS6012 and
6014 will no longer be pre-requisites for admission to the PCLL Programme
with effect from 2021-22. Students may still take the two courses as
elective courses offered under the JD Programme.
[e]  |  Students who
have completed LAWS1041 and 1042 in their first three years of studies in
the BBA-JD programme will be exempted from LAWS6021.
[f]  |  Students who
have completed LAWS2131 and 2132 in their first three years of studies in
the BBA-JD programme will be exempted from LAWS6023.
[g]  |  Students who
have completed LAWS1020, 1030, 1041, 1042, 2131 and 2132 are required to
complete a minimum of 63 units in order to fulfill the programme
requirement.

year to complete the JD degree, students must achieve a minimum CGPA (to be

fulfillment of the requirements specified in the relevant study scheme. * Students who choose any one of the

Marketing, or (vi) Big Data and
Quantitative Marketing are
required to complete a minimum of 73 units in order to fulfill the Major

1030, 1041, 1042, 2131 and 2132 are required to complete a minimum of 63
units in order to fulfill the programme requirement.
